items.title: Compliment
items.meaning: To praise someone
items.example: He complimented her dress

items.title: Complement
items.meaning: To complete or enhance something
items.example: The scarf complemenrs her outfit

items.title: Empathy
items.meaning: Ability to understand and feel what another person is experiencing
items.example: She showed empathy by listening to her friend without judging

items.title: Sympathy
items.meaning: Feeling sorry or concerned for someone without fully sharing their emotions
items.example: She expressed sympathy for her friend after the loss of her pet

items.title: Apathy
items.meaning: Lack of interest,concern,or emotion toward someone else's situation
items.example: His apathy towards his studies worried his parents

items.title: Further
items.meaning: Refers to additional or extra,or to continue
items.example: I need to discuss further before making a decision

items.title: Farther
items.meaning: Refers to physical distance
items.example: The park is farther than I thought from my house

items.title: Expect
items.meaning: To believe something will happen
items.example: I expect good results this year

items.title: Accept
items.meaning: To receive or agree to something
items.example: She accepted the job offer happily

items.title: Except
items.meaning: To leave out or not include
items.example: Everyone came except John

items.title: Affect
items.meaning: To influence
items.example: Lack of sleep can affect your mood

items.title: Effect
items.meaning: Result or outcome
items.example: The medicine had a good effect on her health

items.title: Impact
items.meaning: Strong effect or influence
items.example: The speech had a big impact on the audience

items.title: Emigrate
items.meaning: To leave your own country to live in another
items.example: She emigrated from India to Canada

items.title: Immigrate
items.meaning: To come into a new country to live there
items.example: He immigrated to Australia for work

items.title: Migrate
items.meaning: To move from one place to another
items.example: Many people migrate to cities for better opportunities

items.title: Ellicit
items.meaning: To bring out or draw out
items.example: The teacher's question elicited a thoughtful answer

items.title: Illicit
items.meaning: Illegal or forbidden
items.example: They were arrested for illicit drug trade

items.title: Explicit
items.meaning: Very clear and direct;leaving now doubt
items.example: She gave explicit instructions for the task

items.title: Through
items.meaning: From one side to the other
items.example: She walked through the door

items.title: Though
items.meaning: However
items.example: It was raining,though we went out

items.title: Thorough
items.meaning: Complete or detailed
items.example: She did a thorough job on the project

items.title: Assure
items.meaning: To remove doubt
items.example: She assured me that everything would be fine

items.title: Ensure
items.meaning: To make sure something happens
items.example: Please ensure the door is locked

items.title: Insure
items.meaning: To protect financially against loss or damage
items.example: They insured the car against accident

items.title: Optimist
items.meaning: A person who expects positive outcomes and focuses on the good
items.example: This is a setback,not the end

items.title: Pessimist
items.meaning: A person who expects negative outcomes and focuses on problems
items.example: Nothing ever works out for me

items.title: Personal
items.meaning: Related to an indivdual or private life
items.example: Please do not use your personal phone during office hours

items.title: Personnel
items.meaning: People employed in an organisation; Staff
items.example: Only authorisd personnel are allowed beyond this point

items.title: Peak
items.meaning: The highest point or maximum level
items.example: She reached the peak of her career at just 35

items.title: Peek
items.meaning: To take a quick or secret look
items.example: He took a peek at the surprise gift

items.title: Pique
items.meaning: To stimuate interest or curiosity
items.example: The trailer piqued my interest

items.title: Allusion
items.meaning: An inirect reference to something
items.example: His speech made an allusion to Romeo and Juliet

items.title: Iluusion
items.meaning: Something that appears real but is actually false
items.example: The magician created the illusion of disappearing

items.title: Delusion
items.meaning: A strong belief in something that is not true
items.example: He was under the delusion that he couldn't fail

items.title: Eminent
items.meaning: Famous,respected or highly regarded
items.example: She is an eminent cardiologist known worldwide

items.title: Imminent
items.meaning: About to happen very soon
items.example: Rain is imminent

items.title: Prominent
items.meaning: Important,noticeable or well-known
items.example: She plays a prominent role in the company

items.title: Cite
items.meaning: To quote,mention or refernce as evidence
items.example: Please cite your sources in the report

items.title: Site
items.meaning: A place or location
items.example: The construction site is closed

items.title: Sight
items.meaning: Vision or something seen
items.example: He lost his sight after the accident

items.title: Apprehend
items.meaning: Arrest or catch
items.example: The police apprehended the thief

items.title: Comprehend
items.meaning: To understand
items.example: I finally comprehend the concept

items.title: Reprehend
items.meaning: Strongly criticize
items.example: The manager reprehended the mistake

items.title: Persecute
items.meaning: To treat someone cruelly or unfairly,often because of religion,race or belief
items.example: Many people were persecuted for their religious beliefs

items.title: Persecute
items.meaning: To legal action against someone in court
items.example: The state decided to prosecute the company for fraud

items.title: Execute
items.meaning: To carry out a plan
items.example: The team executed the stratergy perfectly
